cfg.plan: Your GitHub Copilot plan. Determines the included monthly AI Credits allowance used for budget gauges. You can also change the allowance directly in the dashboard.
cfg.plan.business: Copilot Business — 1,900 AI Credits per user per month
cfg.plan.businessPromo: Copilot Business promotional period (Jun–Aug 2026) — 3,000 AI Credits per user per month
cfg.plan.enterprise: Copilot Enterprise — 3,900 AI Credits per user per month
cfg.plan.enterprisePromo: Copilot Enterprise promotional period (Jun–Aug 2026) — 7,000 AI Credits per user per month
cfg.includedCredits: Included AI Credits per month (only used when plan is 'custom').
cfg.monthlyBudget: Optional personal monthly budget in USD (0 = disabled). You get a warning when the month-to-date spend crosses the warning threshold.
cfg.warnAt: Warn when month-to-date usage exceeds this percentage of the included credits (or monthly budget, if set).
cfg.statusBar: Show month-to-date spend in the status bar.
cfg.extraRoots: Additional VS Code 'workspaceStorage' roots to scan (absolute paths). Standard locations of VS Code, Insiders and VSCodium are detected automatically.
cfg.claudeCode: Include Claude Code usage (~/.claude/projects) in per-repository costs. Claude Code spend never counts against the Copilot allowance gauge.
cfg.copilotCli: Include GitHub Copilot CLI usage (~/.copilot/session-state) in per-repository costs.
cfg.estimation: When exact token usage is not available in logs, estimate token usage from chat session content. Estimated entries are clearly marked (~est).
cfg.charsPerToken:
Characters-per-token ratio used **only for `~est` estimated entries** (sessions whose logs carry no exact token counts).

LLM tokenizers average about **4 characters per token for English text and code** — that is the industry rule of thumb and the default here. Languages with diacritics tend to ~3–4, CJK languages to ~1–2 characters per token.

Estimates derived this way are typically within **±20–30 %** of the real token count — good enough to compare repositories and spot trends, not good enough for invoicing. Whenever exact counts exist in the logs, they are used instead and this setting has no effect.
cfg.priceOverrides: Override model pricing (USD per 1M tokens). Example: `{ "gpt-5.5": { "input": 5, "cachedInput": 0.5, "output": 30 } }`
cfg.refreshInterval: How often to rescan usage logs in the background. File watchers trigger immediate refreshes on top of this.
cfg.projectGroups: Group repositories into named projects. Example: `{ "MyProduct": ["acme/frontend", "acme/backend", "acme/e2e-tests"] }`. Groups appear in the dashboard with aggregated costs and a summary invoice PDF with a per-repository breakdown.
cfg.creditAlerts: Absolute AI-credit thresholds (e.g. [2500, 5000]). Each fires a notification once per month when month-to-date Copilot usage crosses it.
cfg.starredRepos: Starred repositories — shown in a dedicated section at the top of the dashboard for a quick overview. Toggle the star directly in the repository table.
